title: Beginner’s Guide to Anichess Spells by Carameru
content:
If you already know how to play chess, the hardest part in Anichess is usually learning when to use spells and how to manage mana properly. This guide focuses on beginner-friendly spells that are simple, flexible, and easy to understand.

---

# Spell Tier List (After Update)
This tier list is based on overall consistency, flexibility, and usefulness for most players after the latest update. Some spells may perform differently depending on playstyle and matchup.

![image|700x700](https://i.ibb.co/dsCF0jWc/Slip.png)

| Tier | Spell |
| -------- |------- |
|  **S**      |   **Slip**       |
|      **S**    |    **Queen Time**     |
|      A    |   Plasma Beam       |
|       A   |    Imposter      |
|      A    |     Transcendence     |
|      A    |    Juke      |
|      A    |      Tactical Necessity    |
|      A    |    Parkour Mech      |
|       A   |      House Recruit    |
|      A    |       Shift   |
|      B    |    Power Infusion      |
|      B    |   Pengu Blast       |
|       B   |       Pounce and Purr   |
|        B  |     Pengu Dive     |
|       B   |     Bish-Hop 2.0     |
|      C   |      Shot in the Dark (SITD)    |

---
# Good Starter Spells
These spells are recommended for newer players because they’re easier to use and consistently useful in most games.


| Spell | Why It's Good |
| -------- | -------- |
|     Queen Time     |  Strong offensive pressure and easy to understand.        |
|    Slip      |   Great survivability and tempo control.       |
|      Juke    |    Reliable repositioning and tactical value.    |
|      Parkour Mech    |   Fun mobility spell with creative plays.       |
|       House Recruit   |  Helps with defense and board control.        |
|    Shift      |    Flexible positioning tool that can fix mistakes quickly.      |

---

# Recommended Starter Spell Sets
![image|150x150](https://i.ibb.co/sJjjqdLL/Queen-Time.png)![image|150x150](https://i.ibb.co/7xBT7TTh/Parkour-Mech-50.png)![image|150x150](https://i.ibb.co/LXw6YGLc/House-Recruit-1-50.png)
## Queen Time + Parkour Mech + House Recruit
### A beginner-friendly aggressive setup.

Why it works:

- Queen Time creates pressure
- Parkour Mech gives mobility and tactical options
- House Recruit helps stabilize your board

Good for players who like active attacking while staying safe.

----
![image|150x150](https://i.ibb.co/dsCF0jWc/Slip.png)![image|150x150](https://i.ibb.co/sJjjqdLL/Queen-Time.png)![image|150x150](https://i.ibb.co/LXw6YGLc/House-Recruit-1-50.png)
## Slip + Queen Time + House Recruit
### A balanced and safer beginner setup.

Why it works:

- Slip helps with survivability and mana tempo
- Queen Time gives strong offensive pressure
- House Recruit improves defense and positioning

Good for players who want a safer and more consistent playstyle.

---

# Common Mistakes 
## 1. Using spells at the wrong timing
A lot of beginners either spam all their spells early or save them forever. Good spell timing is one of the biggest skills in Anichess.

### Sometimes holding a spell is stronger than instantly casting it.

## 2. Ignoring the opponent’s spell set
### Always check the opponent’s spell set before the game starts.

Unlike normal chess, default openings don’t always work in every match because different spell sets completely change how players approach the game.

Example:
- Aggressive spell sets may punish early castling
- Mobility spells can bypass normal defenses
- Control spells can punish overextension

Adapt first before autopiloting your opening.

## 3. Playing pure chess until late game
Some new players completely ignore spells and only play normal chess until the endgame.

### In Anichess, spells are part of the strategy. If you never use them, you’re basically playing at a disadvantage.

## 4. Applying normal chess openings too strictly
Opening principles still help:
- Control the center
- Develop pieces
- Protect your king

But not every normal chess principle applies perfectly because spells can completely change positions instantly.

### Always respect possible spell threats.

--- 
# 11 Tips for Playing Anichess

## 1. Learn spell counters

The stun effect from Pounce and Purr can be negated using:
- Imposter
- Bish-Hop 2.0
- Pengu Dive

You can also break the stun pressure by immediately creating your own threat:
- Attack the enemy king
- Attack an important piece
- Force a response

### Knowing spell interactions helps a lot against aggressive players.

## 2. Don’t underestimate Shot in the Dark
Even if Shot in the Dark isn’t very common in top player spell sets anymore, the threat it creates is still dangerous.

The invisible attack can feel like a 50/50 guess, but experienced players usually avoid it by:

- Anticipating where the invisible threat might be
- Reading the opponent’s setup
- Staying one step ahead before the spell activates

### If you dislike the randomness, the best counter is prevention and positioning before it happens.

## 3. Queen Time is strongest on Knights
Queen Time is already one of the strongest spells in the game, but many players believe it becomes even more dangerous when used on a Knight.
Why?
- Knights are naturally unpredictable
- Queen Time increases pressure instantly
- The movement patterns become harder to defend against

### A Queen Time Knight can easily create surprise attacks and tactical forks if not respected.

## 4. Study top players
### One of the fastest ways to improve is reviewing stronger players.

Go to the leaderboard, click their username, and review:
- Their openings
- Spell sets
- Spell timing
- Positioning choices

You’ll start understanding why certain spell combinations work.

## 5. Play fast with purpose
### Fast moves are good, but random fast moves are not.

Try to play quickly while still having:
- A plan
- Mana awareness
- Spell timing
- Piece coordination

## 6. Be careful with castling
In normal chess, early castling is usually safe.

### In Anichess, castling can sometimes walk directly into spell setups or tactical traps.

Always check the following before castling:
- Enemy spell range
- Mobility spells
- Possible burst damage 

## 7. The 50-move rule still exists
Just like normal chess, the 50-move rule still applies in Anichess.

## 8. No check or stalemate
Anichess works differently from traditional chess.

- No check system
- No stalemate

### Always stay alert because attacks can happen instantly through spells.

## 9. Draws are rare, but repetition still draws

Draws don’t happen often in Anichess because spells create chaos and aggressive opportunities.

However, repetition still counts as a draw.

## 10. Always count mana
Many games are decided by mana management.

Before committing to a strategy, ask:
- Do I have enough mana?
- Can the opponent counter this?
- Am I wasting resources too early?

### Mana management wins games in Anichess.

## 11. Use Training Mode first

If you’re unfamiliar with spells, go to:
- Training Mode
- Get a friend to play custom mode with 1 spell

This helps you:
- Learn spell effects
- Understand cooldowns/casts
- Practice combos safely
- Get comfortable before ranked matches

--- 

# Final Note
Don’t worry too much about mastering every spell immediately. Focus first on understanding positioning, mana usage, and timing. Most improvement comes naturally after playing more games.
